summary: A two-week interdisciplinary laboratory and festival held in Yerevan, Armenia, bringing together sound artists, musicians, visual artists, and performers to explore the intersection of Armenian classical heritage and contemporary experimental practice. The program included workshops on performance theory, folk recordings as digital instruments, and notation systems, alongside collaborative creation sessions that resulted in new works, installations, and music releases. With 1,000 on-site and 2 million online participants, the project fostered international artistic exchange and strengthened Armenia's emerging cultural infrastructure. Key outcomes included an album based on Armenian Khaz notation research, collaborative installations at the State Philharmonia, and expanded professional networks connecting Armenian artists with European cultural policy frameworks.

pros: Strong cultural originality with documented boundary-breaking outcomes (improvisation firsts, cross-disciplinary work, architectural mapping innovation)
Direct linking to Armenian heritage research (khaz notation) anchors the project in meaningful cultural context (album)
Multiple local partnerships with named Armenian artists and organizations embedded in ecosystem
Structured research methodology (observe-research-implement) applied to laboratory design
cons: Audience create and cultural problem not explicitly articulated beyond infrastructure gaps
Co-authorship power dynamics and formal partnership structures on co-design with school partners unclear
Research sources and step-by-step process methodology underdeveloped in artifacts
Contextual claims about Armenian music scene lack supporting data or evidence

pros: Strong market integration with proven distribution (Bandcamp release) and exceptional digital reach (2M online audience)
Demonstrated revenue potential through album sales and documented commercial outputs
Multiple local partnerships with named Armenian artists and organizations providing employment-adjacent opportunities
Financial model of methodology coupled with integrated local economic support
cons: Financial sustainability model unclear beyond singular monetization moment
Financial innovation remains surface-level without structural difference from established models

pros: Exceptional community co-creation with participant-initiated collaborations and public outputs
Strong mechanisms for inclusion of Armenian women, underrepresented participant transformation into ongoing collaborators
Meaningful multicultural engagement bringing Armenian heritage with international precedents
Bilingual operation with diverse stakeholder engagement
cons: No documented accessibility provisions for physical, economic, or digital dimensions
Limited evidence of marginalized voices or systematic outreach
No safeguards against co-design imposition or sponsorship dynamics

pros: Strong contemporary art forms with multiple radical outputs (installation, mapping, digital release)
Clear curatorial methodology with documented participant transformation
Credible team with international partnerships and realized collaborations
Policy-focused critical discourse through review sessions and international exchange
cons: Missing project budget or workflow implementation specifics
No published critique loops on open-call format and structural support systems

pros: Concrete heritage research outputs (Khaz notation album) demonstrates structured engagement with Armenian intangible heritage
Methodological approach to folk recordings provides model for heritage integration
Real-time interactive performance components demonstrate live transformation of traditional materials into public-facing work
cons: No documented partnerships with heritage bodies or formal custodians of Armenian musical tradition
Site-meaningfulness and venue choice versus traditional/environmental resonance not articulated
Heritage preservation component focuses on reinterpretation rather than conservation
No location choice rationale or environmental criteria documented

pros: Strong multi-platform digital presence with completed artifacts (Bandcamp, YouTube, Google Drive)
Technological innovation through architectural mapping and algorithm-based sound transformation
Real-time interactive performance components demonstrated
Accessible distribution through open channels and social media integration
cons: Limited documentation of R&D technical specifications or novel interaction mechanisms
No evidence of sustained user participation loops or interactive online platforms
Overall 2M online audience suggests reach but audience participation mechanisms underspecified

pros: Exceptional trackable partner portfolio with confirmed artists from Georgia, Russia, China, Germany, Italy
Deep multi-cultural representation evidenced through co-created outputs (installations, albums, collaborations)
Transferable cross-cultural exchange model and concrete open-call and co-production structures
Transferability claims with documented replication interest and artistic infrastructure
cons: Transferability remains conceptual with no concrete replication agreements or defined long-term plans
No evidence of meaningful pedagogy mechanisms or learning outcomes for international participants
Documented relationship building shows networking but not lasting infrastructure for follow-on production structures
Institutional relationships lack long-term partnership mechanics, continuous coverage, or governance detail

pros: Exceptional skill transfer outcomes with multiple documented cases of participants acquiring new competencies and producing concrete works
Strong educational design with confirmed project instruction and structured pedagogical approaches across disciplines
Demonstrated ecosystem impact beyond immediate participants, with ripple effects into broader Armenian cultural infrastructure
Institutional partnerships lock formal agreements in defined long-term roles beyond the laboratory period
No evidence of governance, community infrastructure, or funding mechanisms for ongoing sustainability
Single-event format without documented infrastructure for ongoing community
Zero educational or capacity building components in project design
Exceptional active engagement evidenced by participant breakthroughs (first improvisation, cross-disciplinary creation)
Strong cross-disciplinary inclusivity allowing artists to work outside habitual roles
Structured participatory formats (Round Robin, Total Jam) with documented creative mechanism
cons: Dialogue depth not evidenced beyond session descriptions; no documented feedback loops or published outcomes
Process transparency limited: internal structure exists but not communicated publicly or in application materials

pros: Exceptional media traction with 2M online reach and comprehensive documentation assets

cons: No evidence of risk mitigation protocols for complex logistical events with international institutions
Absence of communication strategy for managing expectations around unevenly-funded national partnerships
Risk mitigation planning for potential disruption or criticism not addressed in documentation
